Description: Assessment rules for Exchange Online mail security, authentication, anti-malware, email authentication records, connector security, admin role hygiene, and audit logging.

Rules.SecureScoreMapping: Enable modern authentication for Exchange Online
Rules.Description: Checks that OAuth2 modern authentication is enabled so that MFA and Conditional Access policies apply to Outlook and other mail clients. Without this, Basic auth bypasses all MFA controls.
Verifies that the Exchange Online anti-malware filter policy has file type filtering and Zero-Hour Auto Purge (ZAP) enabled, with admin notification configured for detected malware.

Rules.SecureScoreMapping: Enable DKIM signatures for your domain
Rules.Description: Checks that DKIM signing is enabled for all custom accepted domains. DKIM cryptographically signs outbound email, preventing spoofing and enabling strong DMARC enforcement.

Rules.SecureScoreMapping: Set up DMARC for your email domains
Rules.Description: Performs DNS checks to verify SPF TXT records include Exchange Online mail servers and DMARC records exist at _dmarc.{domain} for all custom accepted domains. Flags DMARC p=none as a warning.

Rules.SecureScoreMapping: Do not allow email forwarding to external domains
Rules.Description: Checks the outbound spam filter AutoForwardingMode setting. Should be Off to prevent compromised mailboxes from silently forwarding email externally — a primary BEC exfiltration technique.
Checks that partner outbound connectors enforce TLS (TlsSettings != None) and partner inbound connectors require TLS (RequireTls = true) to encrypt email in transit with partner organizations.
Verifies that the outbound spam filter policy is configured to notify administrators when an account exceeds spam thresholds, providing early warning of credential compromise.
Checks the number of members in the Organization Management role group. Excessive membership increases the attack surface and the risk of mail flow misconfiguration or data exfiltration.

Checks that organization-level mailbox audit logging is enabled (AuditDisabled = false). Microsoft enables this by default since 2019 but it can be manually disabled, removing the forensic trail for mailbox access.
Verifies that Unified Audit Log ingestion is enabled (UnifiedAuditLogIngestionEnabled = true), providing a centralized record of user and admin activity across Microsoft 365 services.
